reasoning: BBB corporate spreads experienced a recent spike from around 1.14 in late March to a peak of 1.49 on April 9, retreating slightly to 1.46 by April 11. Credit spreads typically show mean-reverting behavior following a sharp short-term widening without a major crisis, meaning it is more likely they will decrease from this local peak than continue increasing. Additionally, the recent historical baseline is between 1.10 and 1.25, heavily suggesting downward mean reversion. Applying the instruction to increase uncertainty closer to 0.5 for more distant dates, I set the probabilities starting safely below 0.5 to reflect the likelihood of retreating from the elevated current value, and gradually approaching 0.5 over longer horizons.
